ai_analysis.summary: Clients are returning due to persistent structural barriers including unemployment/underemployment, ongoing housing instability, and unexpected life disruptions (visa delays, health crises, family separations). Many face compounding vulnerabilities where initial assistance addresses immediate needs but underlying circumstances—such as lack of stable employment, disability, or family crises—remain unresolved, requiring repeated support.

themes.label: Employment Gaps & Underemployment
themes.description: Clients returning due to partners/household members unable to find stable work, job transitions, or working insufficient hours. Initial assistance helped temporarily but structural unemployment or underemployment persists, requiring recurring support.

themes.label: Housing & Accommodation Instability
themes.description: Repeated requests for rent assistance, bond payments, and housing-related expenses. Clients struggle with ongoing housing costs or face temporary housing gaps between transitions, requiring multiple assistance cycles.

themes.label: Vulnerability & Crisis Situations
themes.description: Clients experiencing domestic violence, mental health needs, visa/migration delays, or experiencing homelessness. These situations create complex, multi-layered needs that extend beyond single financial interventions.

themes.label: Essential Living Costs & Basic Needs
themes.description: Repeated requests for utilities, food assistance, and essential bills. Clients lack sufficient income to cover baseline living expenses consistently, particularly when single-income or supporting children with limited work availability.
